vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but requires immediate revision based on visual evidence.

inventory_reconciliation.p2: Systemic Loss (Black Pot, Mexican Mint appears absent or fully desiccated)
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Present (Black Pot, Pothos, White Rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss (Shared space with p2, no distinct Silver Guest specimen identified)

plant_audit.p1: String of Nickels: Showing signs of chlorosis and potential dehydration; foliage is sparse.
plant_audit.p2: Mexican Mint: No viable green tissue detected. Pot appears to contain only substrate.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos: One primary leaf remains, showing significant yellowing and necrosis at the tip. The white rabbit anchor is stable.
plant_audit.p4: Silver Guest: Not detected; likely lost due to environmental stress or competition.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white debris (likely perlite or mineral salt buildup) on the surface of p3. No fungal activity detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: Progressive decline observed. The Pothos (p3) has transitioned from a healthy green to a chlorotic, necrotic state. The Mexican Mint (p2) has vanished from the visible surface area.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. The lack of turgor pressure in the remaining Pothos leaf and the total loss of the Mexican Mint suggest severe water stress or root-zone failure.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit figurine is confirmed as a stable anchor. No new introductions found.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The Pothos in the black pot (p3) is the only remaining specimen showing any structural integrity, though it is clearly dying. The other pots are effectively barren or contain only dead organic matter. Immediate intervention (hydration and soil amendment) is required to prevent total system collapse.
